title: crepidiastrum denticulatum
intro: Crepidiastrum denticulatum, also known as the toothed crepidiastrum, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, among others. In terms of herbal preparations, the roots of the crepidiastrum denticulatum plant are often dried and made into teas or infusions, while the leaves can be used in salads or as a cooking green.
